The narratives must follow the chronological order of events in the book.

1. The team arrives at the cruise ship and is briefed on the plan — this happens early in the story (Chapter 1–2).
2. Petra encounters the elderly tourists and requests the diary — this happens in Chapter 6, after she has been on a break.
3. Rachael and Petra visit the longhouse — this happens in Chapter 11, after Petra has the diary.
4. Petra bursts out of the longhouse into debris — this happens in Chapter 14, at the end of the longhouse scene.

So the correct chronological order is: 1 → 3 → 4 → 2.

Wait — narrative 2 is "Petra bursts out of the longhouse into a cloud of debris" — this is the climax, after the longhouse visit.

Narrative 3 is "The narrator encounters two elderly tourists inside a cabin, and requests the woman to hand over Constable Paniula's diary" — this happens before the longhouse visit.

Narrative 4 is "Rachael and Petra visited the reconstructed Viking longhouse" — this happens after the diary request.

So order: 1 (arrival) → 3 (diary request) → 4 (longhouse visit) → 2 (helicopter chaos)
